title: Rotating Machine Lead Engineer
description:
****What we do at Blykalla****
*Blykalla is a Swedish deep-tech company at the forefront of the nuclear industry, pioneering the development of small modular reactors that provide clean, safe, and reliable energy. Thanks to lead-cooling, we achieve high safety in a very compact format, allowing for a simple and cost-effective design that can be mass-produced. Our unique reactor design will provide safe and green baseload energy that perfectly complements solar and wind powe* 
 r.
   

  

****What you’ll get to do****
 As a Rotating Machine Lead Engineer, you will own the rotating equipment portfolio of the SEALER plant — from the Primary Coolant Pumps (PCPs) submerged in molten lead above 400 °C, where conventional bearing and sealing solutions don't apply, to the secondary-side feedwater and condensate pumps and the full set of auxiliary and safety-related pumps the plant requires. It is a technically demanding portfolio that spans genuinely novel engineering on the primary side and rigorous nuclear-grade discipline throughout:
   

  

* Define functional and design requirements for the full rotating equipment portfolio — PCPs in molten lead, secondary-side feedwater and condensate pumps, and all auxiliary, safety-related, and service pumps.
* Develop design basis documentation and lead trade-off studies on pump configuration, drive arrangement, sealing strategy, and bearing technology.
* Set priorities across the portfolio — distinguishing where Blykalla-specific engineering is warranted from where standard industrial solutions suffice.
* Act as Blykalla's technical counterpart to pump suppliers and engineering partners, challenging design choices and steering solutions to fit plant requirements across hydraulics, sealing, materials, and maintainability.
* Define the scope of hydraulic, rotordynamic, and structural analyses; brief specialists; and critically review their outputs. Use hand calculations to sanity-check results.
* Own supplier work packages: evaluate proposals, lead design reviews, and keep pump development aligned with plant requirements and schedule.
* Support test rig development when needed to advance tailor-made pumping solutions.
* Collaborate across internal teams and with academic and industrial partners.


****Who’ll you’ll get to work with****
 The Rotating Machine Lead Engineer sits in the Fluid Systems Engineering Team within Blykalla, and reports to Federico Barbano, the Manager of Fluids Engineering. We are a diverse team that is passionate about our work and Blykalla
 *.*
****Who you are****
 You will be part of a highly skilled team with a can-do attitude. We value personnel chemistry and strive to create a workplace where you enjoy coming to. There are however a few things that we think will help for you to succeed in this role, these are:
   

  

* More than 5 years of professional experience as a rotating equipment engineer.
* Degree in mechanical, energy, nuclear, or a closely related discipline.
* A meaningful portion of your experience at a pump manufacturer or rotating equipment OEM, ideally in a role combining hydraulic and mechanical design with workshop and field interface, or equivalent experience as a discipline lead on the operator or EPC side.
* Strong working knowledge of centrifugal and/or axial pump design, including rotordynamics, bearing selection, mechanical seals or seal-less concepts, and applicable design codes and standards such as API 610, API 682, ASME III, PED, or RCC-M.
* Hydraulic and mechanical fundamentals sufficient to steer and critically review calculations performed by specialists, including the ability to perform hand calculations on head, NPSH, power, and basic rotordynamics to sanity-check results.
* Comfort writing clear technical specifications, reviewing supplier deliverables critically, and managing a multi-vendor technical dialogue from concept through qualification.
* Familiarity with CAD tools and engineering software.
* Fluent in English, spoken and written.
* Fluency in Swedish is a plus.


**Meritorious skills:**
* Hands-on experience with heavy liquid metal pumps (lead, lead-bismuth, sodium).
* Experience with reactor coolant pumps in light-water reactors.
* Experience with high-pressure feedwater or boiler feed pumps in conventional or nuclear steam plants.
* Familiarity with magnetic drives, canned-motor pumps, or other seal-less concepts.
* Experience with rotordynamic, CFD, or FEA analyses; the priority is the ability to define, commission, and review such analyses rather than execute them.
* Prior experience coordinating multidisciplinary design work or managing supplier technical interfaces.


 If you are not sure that you are 100% qualified but are up to the challenge, we encourage you to apply.
   

  

**Location**
 This position is for our office in Stockholm.
   

  

**So, what do you think?**
 Take the chance to be at the forefront of revolutionizing nuclear power. Join our team as Rotating Machine Lead Engineer and help shape the energy landscape of tomorrow. Apply today with your resume and a cover letter highlighting your relevant experience and achievements.
   

  

 We are proud to foster an inclusive workplace free from discrimination. We strongly believe that diversity of experience, perspectives, and background will lead to a better environment for our employees and a better product. This is something we value deeply and we encourage everyone to be a part of changing the way the world thinks about power supply!
   

  

**We do not accept applications via email.**
 We are not open to external help from recruitment agencies.

title: Master Data Automation Engineer at Oatly
description:
**WHY OATLY?**
--------------


Across the world people’s consumption habits and diets are evolving to be more plant-based. Enter Oatly, the #1 oat based dairy alternative producer in the world with products spanning multiple categories.


We’re determined on being part of the discussion on the long-term future of the food and beverage industry. To keep this movement going, we need more brilliant minds to come work with us to keep the momentum going and the Oatly flowing.


Our sole purpose as a company is to make it easy for people to eat better and live healthier lives without recklessly taxing the planet's resources in the process.


Sustainability, nutritional health and trust are at the heart of what we do. We are building a culture that constantly upgrades each other so we can upgrade the planet. This is a company that shows great respect and a fearless approach to make positive change. Does this feel exciting? Well, this is where you come in.


WHAT WE NEED YOU TO DO


Your mission is simple but impactful: make our master data smarter, more reliable, and ready to drive better decisions across Oatly. You’ll play a key role in our digital journey, helping turn data into insights and automation into real business value.


From designing data pipelines to supporting AI-driven capabilities, you’ll work across teams and systems to improve how we operate, every single day.


More specifically, you will:

* Ensure high-quality master data across enterprise systems like ERP (D365) and EDI platforms
* Design, build, and maintain secure and scalable data pipelines using modern tools and best practices
* Drive automation and contribute to making AI a core capability within Oatly
* Collaborate with developers, product owners, and business stakeholders to deliver smart, data-driven solutions
* Support agile ways of working, facilitating collaboration and continuous improvement
* Contribute to and improve our data governance practices and processes
* Support CI/CD pipelines and DevOps practices to enable efficient and reliable delivery
* Work closely with master data stewards to ensure strong data ownership and consistency
* Support compliance efforts (including SOX) and participate in audits when needed


WHAT WE NEED YOU TO BRING TO THE TABLE


To thrive in this role, we believe you have:

* A B.Sc. in Computer Science, Informatics, or a related field
* +2 years of experience as an Automation Engineer or in a similar role
* Experience with technologies such as Microsoft Azure, Power Platform (Automate, Apps, Power BI, Dataverse), and DevOps
* Familiarity with modern data practices including CI/CD, version control (GIT), and automated testing
* Fluency in English


Bonus points if you have:

* Experience with Python or another object-oriented programming language
* A passion for data engineering, automation, and digital transformation


WHO YOU ARE


We think you might be the kind of person who gets a little spark from making things work better than they did yesterday


You’re just as comfortable taking ownership and running with something yourself as you are teaming up with others to figure things out together because you know the best ideas rarely happen in isolation.


You communicate in a way that makes people lean in, not tune out, and you turn technical complexity and the story behind the data into something people can actually understand and use.


Most importantly, you’re not done learning (and honestly, you hope you never are). You care about building smarter, smoother ways of working and you get a real kick out of seeing that make a difference.


YOUR APPLICATION


Last day of application: 30th of June


We treat all candidates equally. If you are interested, please apply through our application system; any correspondence should come from there. This ensures a smooth and fair experience for everyone.


And hey, no one ticks every single box. If this feels like your kind of challenge, we’d love to hear from you.


Love Oatly XOXO


#LI-TA1

title: Data Scientist
description:
**Mission:**


We are seeking a skilled and motivated Data Scientist to join our team and contribute to the development, implementation, and optimization of advanced algorithms and models. Your work will support key teams such as Field Operations, R&D, Flights, Simulation, Autopilot by automating workflows and enhancing decision support, mission planning, and situational awareness capabilities.

**Requirements:**

* **Education**: Bachelor’s or Master’s degree in Computer Science, Data Science, or a related field. A Ph.D. is a plus.
* **Experience**: 3+ years of experience in data science and machine learning modeling, with a focus
* Experience in mission-critical applications such as field operations, situational awareness, or real-time systems.
* Familiarity with tools and frameworks used in geospatial analytics and drone data processing.
* On time series analysis and ideally predictive modeling.
* Strong background in machine learning, data science, and analytics, with hands-on experience in building and deploying models.
* Proven experience working with multi-modal datasets, such as sensor data as time series, GIS data, text, and unstructured data.
* Solid understanding of data pipelines, model evaluation, and production-level deployment.
* Excellent problem-solving skills and the ability to work in a fast-paced, collaborative environment.
**Key Responsibilities:**

* Design, build, and refine machine learning models and data-driven solutions to address real-world operational challenges.
* Work across the entire data science lifecycle: from problem scoping and prototyping to data annotation, model training, performance evaluation, and production deployment.
* Process and analyze diverse datasets, including but not limited to drone sensor data, geospatial (GIS) data, unstructured text, and other sensor modalities.
* Collaborate with cross-functional teams, including engineering and domain experts, to ensure seamless integration of data science solutions into production environments.
* Drive innovation through experimentation, research, and continuous improvement of existing models and systems.
* **Algorithm Development:** Design, develop and implement state-of-the-art algorithms and models, within the context of language models.
* **Capability Development:** Realize new AI-based capabilities in areas such as decision support, mission planning, workflow automation, production data support and predictive modeling and maintenance.
* **Model Training and Optimization:** Train and optimize models using vast amounts of textual data, ensuring high performance and accuracy.
* **Data Preprocessing:** Perform data preprocessing tasks such as data cleaning, outlier detection, feature scaling, and feature encoding to prepare datasets for training and evaluation.
* **Research & Innovation:** Stay up to date with the latest advancements in machine learning both classical techniques and deep learning and proactively apply cutting-edge methods to enhance existing models and develop innovative solutions.
* **Collaboration**: Work closely with data engineers, software developers, product managers and other stakeholders to understand project requirements and deliver effective solutions.
* **Performance Evaluation:** Evaluate the performance of models using appropriate metrics and techniques and iteratively improve their accuracy and efficiency.
* **Deployment:** Collaborate with engineering teams to deploy models into production environments and ensure their robustness and scalability.
* **Documentation:** Maintain comprehensive documentation of models, algorithms and processes for future reference and reproducibility.
**Technical Skills:**

**Programming & Data Manipulation:**


* Proficient in Python, with hands-on experience using libraries such as NumPy, Pandas, SciPy, and scikit-learn for data analysis and modeling.
* Strong experience with data preprocessing, including handling of time series, unstructured, and geospatial data.
**Machine Learning & Modeling:**

* Expertise in classical ML techniques (e.g., regression, classification, clustering) using scikit-learn, XGBoost, and other ensemble methods.
* Solid understanding of deep learning architectures for time series analysis, including Transformers, RNNs/LSTMs, and CNNs.
* Experience with model training, evaluation, and fine-tuning in varied computing environments.
**Deep Learning Frameworks:**


* Proficient with frameworks such as PyTorch, TensorFlow, Keras, and TensorRT for building and optimizing deep learning models.
* Experience working with Hugging Face Transformers for sequence modeling and transfer learning tasks.
**Experimentation & Development Tools:**


* Skilled in interactive experimentation environments such as Jupyter Notebooks.
* Familiarity with ML experiment tracking and versioning tools is a plus (e.g., MLflow, Weights & Biases).
**Geospatial & Sensor Data:**

* Experience working with GIS systems, drone sensor data, and multi-modal datasets.
* Familiarity with geospatial libraries and tools such as GeoPandas, Rasterio, QGIS, or similar is a strong advantage.
**Cloud & Deployment:**

* Experience deploying production-grade models, including containerization and REST API integration.
* Familiar with cloud platforms (especially Google Cloud Platform), including ML services, storage, and serverless deployment.
* **Basic understanding of DevOps practices:** version control (Git), CI/CD pipelines, telemetry, monitoring, and model lifecycle management.
* **Analytical Skills:** Excellent analytical and problem-solving skills with the ability to design innovative solutions to complex problems.
* **AI Ethics and Bias Mitigation:** Experience or awareness of AI ethics, fairness and bias mitigation strategies
* **Communication:** Strong verbal and written communication skills, with the ability to explain complex technical concepts to non-technical stakeholders.
* Collaboration: Ability to work effectively in a collaborative, cross-functional team environment.
* **Attention to Detail**: High attention to detail and a commitment to ensuring the accuracy and quality of work.
* **Adaptability**: Ability to thrive in a fast-paced, dynamic environment and manage multiple projects simultaneously.
**What we have to offer you:**

* An excellent work environment and an opportunity to create a real impact in the world;
* A truly high-tech, state-of-the-art engineering company with flat structure and no politics;
* Working with the very latest technologies in Data & AI, including Edge AI, Swarming - both within our software platforms and within our embedded on-board systems;
* Flexible work arrangements;
* Professional development opportunities;
* Collaborative and inclusive work environment;
* Salary compatible with the level of proven experience.
*Pre-Employment Requirements*


Employment offers may be contingent upon successful completion of a background check and reference verification, in accordance with applicable laws and company policy.

*Equal Employment Opportunity*


TEKEVER is an Equal Opportunity Employer. We consider all qualified applicants without regard to race, color, religion, sex (including pregnancy, sexual orientation, gender identity, and gender expression), national origin, age, disability, genetic information, protected veteran status, or any other characteristic protected by federal, state, or local law.


Do you want to know more about us ?


Visit our LinkedIn page at https://www.linkedin.com/company/tekever/

title: Data Scientist | LLM Engineer
description:
**Mission:**


As a Data Scientist specializing in LLM you will be responsible for developing, implementing and optimizing advanced algorithms, models and capabilities that help our teams (e.g. Field Operations) automate their workloads, e.g. in the context of decision support, mission planning and situational awareness. You will work on a variety of projects that involve understanding, processing and generating human language to solve complex problems and create innovative solutions. The ideal candidate will have a strong background in LLMs, machine learning and data science, with a proven track record of successful projects in these domains.

**What will be your responsibilities:**

* **Algorithm Development:** Design, develop and implement state-of-the-art algorithms and models, within the context of language models.
* **Capability Development:** Realize new AI-based capabilities in areas such as decision support, mission planning, workflow automation.
* **Model Training and Optimization**: Train and optimize large language models using vast amounts of textual data, ensuring high performance and accuracy.
* **Data Preprocessing**: Perform data preprocessing tasks such as tokenization, stemming, lemmatization and normalization to prepare datasets for training and evaluation.
* **Research and Innovation:** Stay current with the latest advancements in LLM and Natural Language Processing (NLP) and apply new techniques to improve existing models and develop new solutions.
* **Collaboration:** Work closely with data engineers, software developers, product managers and other stakeholders to understand project requirements and deliver effective solutions.
* **Performance Evaluation:** Evaluate the performance of models using appropriate metrics and techniques and iteratively improve their accuracy and efficiency.
* **Deployment:** Collaborate with engineering teams to deploy models into production environments and ensure their robustness and scalability.
* **Documentation:** Maintain comprehensive documentation of models, algorithms and processes for future reference and reproducibility.
**Profile and requirements:**

* **Education**: Bachelor’s or Master’s degree in Computer Science, Data Science, or a related field. A Ph.D. is a plus.
* **Experience:** 3+ years of experience in data science, with a focus on large language models and NLP.
**Technical Skills:**

* **Strong programming skills in Python,** with experience using NLP and LLM libraries such as spaCy, Hugging Face (Transformers, Datasets, PEFT, TRL) and the major model families (e.g. GPT, Claude, Gemini, Llama, Mistral, Qwen, Gemma) via both API and open weights.
* **Proficiency in deep learning frameworks,** primarily PyTorch (plus Keras/TensorFlow as needed), and familiarity with inference optimisation (quantisation, TensorRT-LLM).
* **Experience with data preprocessing**, curation and tokenisation for LLM workloads, including building and cleaning datasets for fine-tuning and retrieval (chunking, embeddings, deduplication, synthetic data generation).
* **Solid understanding of transformer architectures** and attention, with working knowledge of fine-tuning and alignment techniques (full fine-tuning, LoRA/QLoRA, instruction tuning, RLHF/DPO). Exposure to RNNs and CNNs is a plus rather than a core requirement.
* **Experience training and fine-tuning LLMs** and building RAG and agentic systems, including orchestration frameworks (LangChain, LlamaIndex, LangGraph), vector databases (e.g. Qdrant, Weaviate, pgvector) and tool/function calling.
* **Experience with experimentation and tracking tooling**: Jupyter notebooks plus experiment and prompt tracking (MLflow, Weights & Biases) and LLM evaluation (e.g. Ragas, LangSmith/Langfuse, custom eval harnesses).
* **Familiarity with cloud platforms** (AWS, Azure, Google Cloud) and their AI services, with a focus on Google Cloud (Vertex AI, model garden, managed endpoints).
* **Experience deploying** self-hosted and open-weight LLMs in production, using serving frameworks such as vLLM, TGI, Ollama or llama.cpp, with awareness of GPU sizing, quantisation formats (GGUF, AWQ, GPTQ) and on-prem or airgapped constraints.
* **Working knowledge of MLOps/LLMOps and DevOps practices:** Git, CI/CD, containerisation (Docker, Kubernetes), plus telemetry, monitoring and observability for model and inference performance.
* A**nalytical Skills:** Excellent analytical and problem-solving skills with the ability to design innovative solutions to complex problems.
* **AI Ethics and Bias Mitigation:** Experience or awareness of AI ethics, fairness and bias mitigation strategies, in the context of NLP and LLMs.
* **Communication:** Strong verbal and written communication skills, with the ability to explain complex technical concepts to non-technical stakeholders.
* **Collaboration:** Ability to work effectively in a collaborative, cross-functional team environment.
* Attention to Detail: High attention to detail and a commitment to ensuring the accuracy and quality of work.
* **Adaptability:** Ability to thrive in a fast-paced, dynamic environment and manage multiple projects simultaneously.
**What we have to offer you:**

* An excellent work environment and an opportunity to create a real impact in the world;
* A truly high-tech, state-of-the-art engineering company with flat structure and no politics;
* Working with the very latest technologies in Data & AI, including Edge AI, Swarming - both within our software platforms and within our embedded on-board systems;
* Flexible work arrangements;
* Professional development opportunities;
* Collaborative and inclusive work environment;
* Salary compatible with the level of proven experience.
*Pre-Employment Requirements*


Employment offers may be contingent upon successful completion of a background check and reference verification, in accordance with applicable laws and company policy.

*Equal Employment Opportunity*


TEKEVER is an Equal Opportunity Employer. We consider all qualified applicants without regard to race, color, religion, sex (including pregnancy, sexual orientation, gender identity, and gender expression), national origin, age, disability, genetic information, protected veteran status, or any other characteristic protected by federal, state, or local law.


Do you want to know more about us ?


Visit our LinkedIn page at https://www.linkedin.com/company/tekever/

title: Linux System Engineer till Vaultica!
description:
*Vill du arbeta i en affärskritisk IT‑miljö där stabilitet, säkerhet och tillgänglighet är helt avgörande? Vi söker nu en Linux System Engineer som vill ta tekniskt ägarskap och arbeta operativt med drift, modernisering och vidareutveckling av Linuxbaserad infrastruktur i en internationell datacenterorganisation.*
**Din vardag**
 Som Linux System Engineer arbetar du operativt med drift, administration och vidareutveckling av Linuxbaserad infrastruktur i datacentermiljö. Du har ett helhetsansvar för att systemen är stabila, säkra och väl dokumenterade, samtidigt som du driver förbättringar och successivt reducerar teknisk skuld i befintliga miljöer.
   

  

**Exempel På Arbetsuppgifter**
* Säkerställa stabil och säker drift av Linuxservrar i affärskritiska miljöer
* Installera, konfigurera och patcha operativsystem och tredjepartsapplikationer
* Designa, implementera och vidareutveckla backup och disaster recovery‑lösningar
* Arbeta proaktivt med säkerhet och säkerställa att miljöerna följer interna policys och riktlinjer
* Automatisera drift och underhåll med hjälp av Ansible och shell‑script
* Arbeta strukturerat med versionshantering och konfigurationsflöden i Git
* Övervaka systemhälsa och prestanda samt hantera incidenter och genomföra root cause analysis
* Dokumentera miljöer, tekniska standarder och arbetssätt


 Du samarbetar nära kollegor inom nätverk, säkerhet och drift samt med internationella team inom organisationen. Rollen innebär stort eget ansvar och du blir en viktig teknisk referens inom Linuxområdet. Beredskap/on‑call ingår som en naturlig del av rollen.
   

  

**Vem är du?**
**Vi Söker Dig Som Har**
* Flerårig erfarenhet som Linux System Engineer i produktionsmiljö
* Mycket god Linuxkompetens, gärna i Debian‑baserade miljöer
* Erfarenhet av affärskritiska eller högt tillgängliga system
* Vana av automation med Ansible och/eller shell scripting
* Erfarenhet av Git och strukturerade konfigurationsflöden
* Förståelse för säkerhet, stabil drift och operativa processer
* Flytande i svenska och engelska i tal och skrift – arbetsspråket är främst engelska


 Som person är du trygg i din kompetens och trivs i att ha stor autonomi i din roll. Du är strukturerad, lösningsorienterad och har lätt för att sätta dig in i komplexa tekniska miljöer. Samarbete är en självklar del av ditt arbetssätt och du kommunicerar tydligt och prestigelöst med andra funktioner.
   

  

**Om oss**
 Vaultica är ett internationellt datacenterbolag som driver, utvecklar och bygger datacenter i Norden och Europa. Bolaget befinner sig i en tydlig expansionsfas och IT‑funktionen är affärskritisk. Organisationen är relativt liten i förhållande till den tekniska komplexiteten, vilket innebär korta beslutsvägar, stort individuellt ansvar och goda möjligheter att påverka både teknikval och arbetssätt.
   

  

**DIN ANSÖKAN**
*I denna rekrytering samarbetar företaget* 

*med Wise.*
*Vi tar inte emot ansökningar via mail, utan du söker enkelt med ditt CV eller din LinkedIn-profil på* 
***wise.se***
*. Vi använder kompetensbaserad rekrytering som metod. Det gör vi i vår strävan efter en mer potentialbaserad matchning. Vi ser därför gärna sökanden med olika bakgrunder, åldrar och erfarenheter, eftersom vi vet att en bredare mångfald berikar var och en av oss och gör oss bättre och smartare som organisation.*
*Har du frågor om processen eller rollen? Kontakta då ansvarig rekryteringskonsult Kristin Mörth Shakhovskoy –* 
*kristin.morth@wise.se* 
*eller Gustaf Linder,* 
*gustaf.linder@wise.se* 
*. Vi intervjuar löpande, så rollen kan tillsättas före sista ansökningsdatum.*
*Varmt välkommen med din ansökan!*

title: Senior Engineering Manager
description:
**Craft:**
 Technology & Development
   

  

**Job Description:**
**Senior Engineering Manager, Game Production – Build**
**Your role within our Kingdom**
 We are looking for a Senior Engineering Manager to lead the Build team within Game Production Technology. This team provides the build and continuous delivery capabilities that developers across King rely on every day to build, validate, and ship game technology at scale for hundreds of millions of players around the world.
   

  

 You will combine strong people leadership with the ability to drive technical outcomes across a complex stakeholder landscape. The scope includes build systems, CI/CD pipelines, build automation, pipeline reliability, release-enabling workflows, developer self-service, and the standards that help game teams and engine teams deliver faster and with higher confidence.
   

  

 Your mission is to make building and delivering game software faster, safer, and more reliable: reducing cycle time and failures, improving quality signals, increasing developer self-service, and driving standardization that scales across studios. You should be proactive and self-driven, while partnering closely with game teams, engine teams, Infrastructure, Technical Directors, Engineering Directors, and Game Production leadership.
   

  

**Skills To Create Thrills**
**People leadership and team health**
* Lead, coach, and develop a high-performing Build team; grow senior engineers into strong technical and organizational leaders.
* Create a culture of ownership, psychological safety, accountability, collaboration, and continuous improvement.
* Define the team structure and skillset needed to deliver against product and technology priorities, adapting as priorities evolve.
* Build a strong service and enablement mindset, with clear communication and collaboration inside the team and with partner teams.
* Support hiring, onboarding, retention, feedback, and hands-on career development for engineers.


**Build strategy and delivery**
* Set a clear vision and roadmap for Build and CI/CD capabilities aligned with game production needs and company priorities.
* Translate strategy into executable plans: milestones, resourcing, delivery cadence, risks, dependencies, and measurable success criteria.
* Drive convergence through build standards and golden paths, reducing fragmentation, maintenance cost, and repeated local solutions across studios.
* Partner with game teams, engine teams, Technical Directors, Engineering Directors, and other stakeholders to align priorities and make effective trade-offs.
* Independently contribute to the long-term engineering vision, strategy, and roadmap for your organization.


**Ownership Of Build And CI/CD Capabilities**
* Own the reliability, scalability, usability, and performance of Build and CI/CD systems that support developers across King.
* Improve build pipelines, automation, orchestration, access patterns, cost efficiency, security posture, and developer self-service.
* Ensure architectural and delivery decisions are well understood, clearly communicated, and defended when needed.
* Contribute to build-or-buy evaluations and technical decisions that affect build technology and developer workflows.
* Support release-management evolution by enabling reliable build and delivery workflows for new release strategies.


**Modernization and operational excellence**
* Lead modernization initiatives across build tooling and CI/CD, including migrations, consolidation, and deprecation of legacy solutions.
* Strengthen operational practices: observability, incident management, post-incident learning, preventative reliability work, and clear service ownership.
* Reduce developer toil by eliminating recurring pain points and investing in automation, documentation, and self-service capabilities.
* Use data-driven and data-informed ways of working to prioritize improvements and evaluate impact.
* Contribute to the local Tech culture by exploring ways to improve how we work and how our developer platforms evolve


**Outcomes, metrics, and stakeholder leadership**
* Be proactive and self-driven: continuously identify opportunities, propose improvements, and run lightweight experiments to validate impact and drive change.
* Define and track KPIs that reflect real developer impact, such as build cycle time, queue time, failure rate, flakiness, MTTR, service availability, adoption, and developer satisfaction.
* Communicate clearly with leadership and stakeholders; align priorities, manage trade-offs, and deliver predictable outcomes.
* Build strong partnerships with game teams and engine teams, ensuring the Build roadmap reflects real needs and adoption is successful.
* Focus on outcomes over output, balancing stakeholder alignment with forward momentum.


**About King**
 With a mission of
 *Making the World Playful* 
 , King is a leading interactive entertainment company with more than 20 years of history of delivering some of the world’s most iconic games in the mobile gaming industry, including the world-famous Candy Crush franchise, as well as other mobile game hits such as
 *Farm Heroes Sag* 
 a. King games are played by more than 200 million monthly active users. King, part of Microsoft (NASDAQ: MSFT), has Kingsters in Stockholm, Malmö, London, Barcelona, Berlin, Dublin, San Francisco, New York, Los Angeles and Malta. More information can be found at King.com or by following us on LinkedIn, @lifeatking on Instagram, or @king\_games on X.

title: Application Support Engineer|Java Microservices +Camunda BPMN| Stockholm, Sweden
description:
**Role** 
 –Application Support Engineer
   

  

**Technology** 
 – Java/Spring Boot microservices with Camunda BPMN/DMN, Kubernetes, Docker, CICD
   

  

**Location** 
 – Sweden
   

  

**Business Unit** 
 – ENGNE
   

  

**Compensation** 
 – Competitive (including bonus)
   

  

**Job Description**
 Today, the corporate landscape is dynamic and the world ahead is full of possibilities! None of the amazing things we do at Infosys would be possible without an equally amazing culture, the environment where ideas can flourish and where you are empowered to move forward as far as your ideas will take you.
   

  

 At Infosys, we assure that your career will never stand still, we will inspire you to build what’s next and we will navigate further together. Our journey of learnability, values and trusted relationships with our clients continue to be the cornerstones of our organization and these values are upheld only because of our people.
   

  

**Required**
* Strong experience with Java/Spring Boot microservices.
* Hands on with Camunda BPMN/DMN for workflow and decision automation.
* Experience in resource order orchestration using BPMN workflows.
* Integration skills with REST APIs and Kafka (producers/consumers).
* Knowledge of External Task Worker patterns in Camunda.
* Experience with CI/CD pipelines and automated deployments.
* Hands on with containerization using Docker and Kubernetes.
* Understanding of observability: logs (JSON/ELK/Splunk) and metrics (Prometheus/Grafana).
* Experience in L2/L3 workflow incident handling, debugging failed jobs, retries, and error paths.
* Ability to work in SLA driven operations with on call/rotational support.
* Experience working in Agile/Scrum teams.
* Good communication skills for cross team collaboration (Dev, QA, SRE, Ops)


**Preferred**
* Experience with telecom order/resource orchestration.
* Knowledge of Camunda 8 (Zeebe) and related tools (Operate/Optimize/Task list).
* Familiarity with cloud native architectures and distributed tracing (Open Telemetry/Jaeger).
* Understanding microservices reliability patterns (circuit breaker, retry, compensation).
* Knowledge of network protocols (BGP, OSPF, VLANs, STP) and enterprise network design.


**Personal**
 Besides the professional qualifications of the candidates, we place great importance in addition to various forms personality profile. These include:
   

  

* High analytical skills
* A high degree of initiative and flexibility
* High customer orientation
* High quality awareness
* Excellent verbal and written communication skills


**About Infosys**
 Infosys is a global leader in next-generation digital services and consulting. We enable clients in 50+ countries to navigate their digital transformation. With over three decades of experience in managing the systems and workings of global enterprises, we expertly steer our clients through the many next of their digital journey. We do it by enabling the enterprise with an AI-powered core that helps prioritize the execution of change. We also empower the business with agile digital at scale to deliver unprecedented levels of performance and customer delight. Our always-on learning agenda drives their continuous improvement through building and transferring digital skills, expertise and ideas from our innovation ecosystem.
   

  

 Visit www.infosys.com to see how Infosys can help your enterprise navigate your next.
   

  

**All aspects of employment at Infosys are based on merit, competence and performance. We are committed to embracing diversity and creating an inclusive environment for all employees. Infosys is proud to be an equal opportunity employer**

title: IT Architect - Stockholm | BCG Platinion
description:
Who We Are
   

  

 Do you want to turn the challenges of digitalization into the solutions of the future? Join BCG Platinion in tackling the core IT issues of leading global companies!
   

  

 Deploy customized platforms, architectures, strategies, and organizations to leverage the digital potential of our customers and drive true innovation, with state-of-the-art tools such as cloud computing, AI, blockchain, microservices, and containerization. Add your individual strengths to our unique team, always there to support you. Together with Boston Consulting Group and our clients, we turn digital visions into reality.
   

  

 What You'll Do
   

  

 For our Stockholm office, we are looking for IT Architects who will collaborate with colleagues from BCG Platinion and/or the Boston Consulting Group on local and international client projects! We develop superior IT strategies, concepts, and solutions actively and on site, applying our technical know-how, experience in IT projects, and analytical and conceptual skills.
   

  

 What You'll Bring
   

  

**You can help us make a difference if you**
* have a degree in computer science, (business) informatics, engineering, or a related field
* have approx. two years of relevant experience in IT consulting or software development (part or full time)
* know that modern tools such as machine learning, cyber resilience, DevOps, agile methods, AI, and robotics aren't just buzzwords, but essential to sustainable IT architectures
* have broad technical expertise and interest in technology trends
* feel particularly comfortable in interdisciplinary teams and global contexts and are flexible and willing to travel
* are excellent at understanding and communicating complex topics in English


 Who You'll Work With
   

  

**Shape the world of tomorrow**
* Shape the digital transformation of globally leading companies, ensuring their success by evaluating their capabilities and performing feasibility studies
* Manage small, agile development teams in strategic IT implementation programs, establish modern practices like DevOps and CI/CD, and be in charge of the technical implementation of crucial digital requirements such as cyber resilience
* Analyze, develop, and optimize cutting-edge, customized IT architectures while applying your comprehensive understanding of legacy IT, enterprise solutions, and current market developments and trends such as cloud computing and microservices


 Additional info
   

  

**How You Can Grow With Us**
 We guarantee constant development incentives in an open and rewarding work environment! Together with a dedicated team of talented colleagues, you get to work on new challenges every day. Individual training offers and a culture of open feedback will provide the framework needed to explore and perfect personal strengths. Our modern structures allow focusing on the essence of our work - with easy travel booking, an excellent back-office support and comprehensive Knowledge Center. With our regular office Fridays and legendary events, we nurture a strong and close team network beyond our case work.
   

  

*#BCGPlatinion*
 Boston Consulting Group is an Equal Opportunity Employer. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, age, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity / expression, national origin, disability, protected veteran status, or any other characteristic protected under national, provincial, or local law, where applicable, and those with criminal histories will be considered in a manner consistent with applicable state and local laws.
   

  

 BCG is an E - Verify Employer. Click here for more information on E-Verify.

title: Smart Manufacturing Engineer
description:
Do you want to help build the next generation of truly smart factories?
   

  

 As a
 **Smart Manufacturing Engineer** 
 , you’ll play a key role in designing and implementing modern Industrial IoT architectures with a strong focus on the
 **Unified Namespace (UNS)** 
 . You will work at the
 **intersection of OT and IT** 
 , helping industrial clients transition from isolated systems to real-time, scalable, and standardized data ecosystems.
   

  

 In this role, you will:
   

  

* Work hands-on with UNS architecture in real industrial environments
* Build real-time data flows from PLC to cloud
* Influence the direction of future factory architectures
* Work with modern technologies such as MQTT, Sparkplug B, OPC UA, and edge solutions
* Operate as a technical specialist in a senior, collaborative team


 Your future duties and responsibilities
   

  

* Build Unified Namespace solutions


 Design and implement scalable UNS architectures based on ISA‑95.
   

  

* Develop real-time data pipelines


 Configure MQTT brokers (e.g., HiveMQ, EMQX, Mosquitto), work with Sparkplug B, and build robust event-driven data flows.
   

  

* Integrate OT and IT systems


 Connect PLC, SCADA, MES, and cloud platforms via OPC UA and other industrial protocols.
   

  

* Bridge business and technology


 Translate operational needs into technical solutions, enabling cohesive data flows from factory floor to enterprise.
   

  

* Contribute in workshops and presales


 Participate in design sessions, customer discussions, and—when needed—presales activities.
   

  

****Language Requirements****
 Required qualifications to be successful in this role
   

  

 Fluent English (spoken and written) — required
   

  

 Knowledge of Swedish — meritorious
   

  

**Core Experience:**
 Unified Namespace (UNS):
   

  

 Practical experience designing and implementing UNS solutions aligned with ISA‑95.
   

  

**MQTT & Sparkplug B:**
 Experience working with MQTT brokers (HiveMQ, EMQX, Mosquitto) and topic modeling.
   

  

**PLC Integration & Industrial Systems:**
 Hands-on experience with Siemens, Rockwell, or Beckhoff and working with OPC UA.
   

  

**Industrial Protocols & OT Environments:**
 Familiarity with OPC UA, Modbus, Profinet (or similar) and confidence working in production environments.
   

  

**IIoT & Integration:**
 Experience connecting production systems with enterprise or cloud platforms.
   

  

**CI/CD & Infrastructure as Code:**
 Structured approach to version control and automation.
   

  

**Meritorious**
 Experience with MES and ERP integration
   

  

 Edge computing and containerization (Docker, Kubernetes)
   

  

 Cybersecurity in industrial environments
   

  

 Experience with Ignition, Litmus, Kepware, or similar platforms
   

  

 Want to know more?
   

  

 We look forward to hearing from you! If you have any further questions, feel free to contact Marko Milosavljevic at marko.milosavljevic@cgi.com.
   

  

**Together, as owners, let’s turn meaningful insights into action.**
 Life at CGI is rooted in ownership, teamwork, respect and belonging. Here, you’ll reach your full potential because…
   

  

 You are invited to be an owner from day 1 as we work together to bring our Dream to life. That’s why we call ourselves CGI Partners rather than employees. We benefit from our collective success and actively shape our company’s strategy and direction.
   

  

 Your work creates value. You’ll develop innovative solutions and build relationships with teammates and clients while accessing global capabilities to scale your ideas, embrace new opportunities, and benefit from expansive industry and technology expertise.
   

  

 You’ll shape your career by joining a company built to grow and last. You’ll be supported by leaders who care about your health and well-being and provide you with opportunities to deepen your skills and broaden your horizons.
   

  

 Come join our team—one of the largest IT and business consulting services firms in the world.

title: Systemutvecklare – Java och Cloud
description:
**Open Source, gemenskap och problemlösning driver oss framåt. Nu söker vi fler likasinnade som vill påverka samhällets digitaliseringslösningar, som förenklar din och dina medmänniskors vardag.**
**Vi är ett konsultbolag med rötter och hjärta inom open source. Våra team består av några av branschens främsta experter inom systemutveckling, cloud och driftsättning, och vi delar ständigt kunskap genom techtalks, kompetensgrupper och konferenser som Fosdem och Kubecon.
   

  

 Vi är en arbetsplats för dig som älskar teknik och värderar att ha inflytande över både din och företagets utveckling.**
**Arbetsuppgifter**
 Som konsult hos oss varierar uppdragen över tid, vilket passar dig som gillar nya tekniska miljöer, olika team och att snabbt sätta dig in i nya problem.
   

  

 Gemensamt för uppdragen är att du kommer arbeta med Java fullstackutveckling i hela ekosystemet, från utveckling och test till deploy. Du arbetar med moderna verktyg som Docker, databaser och CI/CD, och kommer hantera lösningar i molntjänster som OpenShift, AWS eller Azure.
   

  

 Vad vår kollega Henrik Karlsson säger om rollen:
   

  

 "Det som är bäst med att vara konsult är att man får erfarenhet av olika branscher och företagsstrukturer. Det som lockade mig till just Redpill Linpro var möjligheten att se att ens arbete har en positiv påverkan på samhället. T.ex. I mitt nuvarande uppdrag får jag vara en del av digitaliseringen av nationella prov. Det handlar om mer än bara teknik. Jag är med och skapar mer rättvisa och säkra provprocesser. Jag arbetar främst med Java, Spring Boot, Angular och PostgreSQL i en containerbaserad miljö med OpenShift och Docker."
   

  

 Vi söker dig som
   

  

* Har några års erfarenhet som fullstackutvecklare med Java och JavaScript, på ramverk som exempelvis Spring Boot, Quarkus, Vert.x, gRPC
* Har erfarenhet av:
+ Docker
+ Databaser
+ CI/CD

* Har erfarenhet av molntjänster (OpenShift, AWS eller Azure)
* Har relevant utbildning inom området
* Behärskar svenska och engelska i tal och skrift


 Meriterande
   

  

* Infrastruktur och orkestrering med exempelvis Kubernetes
* Observability
* Python
* Tidigare erfarenhet av att arbeta som konsult


 Varför Redpill Linpro?
   

  

 Vi har ett tydligt fokus på open source och moderna tekniker, eftersom vi tror att det är avgörande för att bygga ett säkrare och mer hållbart samhälle. Hos oss arbetar du tillsammans med ledande experter inom systemutveckling, cloud och driftsättning i uppdrag där kvalitet och långsiktighet står i centrum.
   

  

 Genom en platt organisation har du reellt inflytande över din vardag och ditt uppdrag. Vi satsar kontinuerligt på kompetensutveckling genom bl.a. techtalks, kompetensgrupper och konferenser, samtidigt som vi erbjuder en stabil och långsiktig arbetsplats med engagerade kollegor och en positiv arbetsmiljö.
   

  

 Intresserad av att bli en del av vårt team?
   

  

 Vi ser fram emot att ta emot din ansökan redan idag! Vi går löpande igenom ansökningar och kommer kontakta dig så snart vi kan.
   

  

**Har du frågor kring tjänsten?** 
 Tveka inte att höra av dig till vårt Talent-team: TA@redpill-linpro.com
   

  

 Redpill Linpro is the leading provider of Open Source services and products in the Nordics. We are located in seven offices with a mission to share knowledge and best practices for a more open society.
   

  

 We are passionate about open solutions, open business models, and an open society where we work together for maximum benefit to everyone. We have got some really skilled co-workers here at Redpill Linpro, particularly within our main areas: API management, development, IT operations, data analytics, customer experience, cloud, DevOps, and automation.
   

  

**May the Source be with you!**

title: System Engineer
description:
Great Tech! Great Team! Great Culture!
   

  

 Standing at the critical junction between your network and your applications, A10 is leading the pack in keeping them secure and enhancing performance. Are you up for the challenge? Join us and make your impact on the future of Sales Team!
   

  

 A10 Networks’
 **System Engineers** 
 act in a pre-sales role promoting A10 product solutions. In this role you’ll perform customer demonstrations explaining features and benefits to customers, assist the customer with network designs, and configure equipment to meet specific customer requirements.
   

  

**Responsibilities**
* Providing technical and sales support for accounts and channel in assigned territory;
* Perform presentations (technical, products, solution) for customers, partners and prospects;
* Assist with the development of formal sales plans and proposals for assigned opportunities;
* Be responsible for POC testing, RFP & technical solutions drafting, competitor analysis, training materials, media soft paper and promotional documentation organizing;
* Internal training for sales and marketing team; external training for Channel partners & key customers;
* Remote and on-site support for customer technical problem
* On-site technical support for key customers technical troubleshooting


**Required Qualifications**
* A Bachelor’s degree in EE or CS (or equivalent)
* Minimum 4 years’ experience in pre-sales
* Knowledge of Layer 4-7 protocols and technologies, such as TCP, UDP, HTTP, DNS and SSL/TLS
* Strong L2/L3 expertise with an emphasis on Network Routing Protocols, such as OSPF/BGP
* In-depth knowledge in multiple areas of specialization: Virtualization, Cloud-Native, Application Acceleration, DDOS mitigation, IPv4 Preservation/IPv6 Migration technologies and Network and Application Security
* Strong operating experience in at least one of the following areas: Application Delivery Controller, Enterprise Applications, Network Security
* Ability to work independently, effective and as a team member
* Fluent in Spanish and English, spoken and written communications
* Available to travel with Europe and occasionally Middle East and Africa
* Ability to travel to the US


**Desirable Skills**
* Competitive knowledge in Layer 4-7 switching including solutions, technology and product offerings
* Knowledgeable and conversant about company, competitive landscape, technologies, solutions, product strengths and weaknesses, opportunities and threats
* Knowledge of protocols and technologies in mobile service provider/ISP, such as CGNAT, Gi/SGi Firewall, GTP Firewall, DDoS Clean Pipe
* Scripting programming experience in Shell or TCL along with Unix/Linux technical experience

title: Integration Subpractice Leader
description:
Choosing Capgemini means choosing a company where you will be empowered to shape your career in the way you’d like, where you’ll be supported and inspired by a collaborative community of colleagues around the world, and where you’ll be able to reimagine what’s possible. Our Cloud & Custom Applications teams focuses on developing tailored cloud solutions, integrating IT systems, and driving business transformation, delivering business-critical results with quality and speed, powered by Generative AI. Capgemini’s Integration & API capability helps clients connect their digital core; applications, data, partners, and platforms, securely and at scale. Join us and help the world’s leading organizations unlock the value of technology and build a more sustainable, more inclusive world!


**Your Role**


We are now looking for an Integration Subpractice Leader to join the leadership team of our Integration business line in Sweden. You will be essential in driving the growth of our Integration business and practice capacity. You will be the people manager for team managers across Stockholm, Göteborg, Helsingborg and Malmö. In your role you will collaborate with our Head of Integrations & APIs to, for example, co-shape our strategy. You will ensure progress in execution of practice growth in and alignment to market demands. As an expert in your field you will be a visible ambassador for our integration and API offerings, and a client-facing leader. You are strong in architecture and will lead and develop our delivery teams, while also contributing part-time in billable engagements.


**Lead & grow the Integration Subpractice in Sweden**


* Lead, coach and develop team managers across multiple locations; set direction, priorities, and execution cadence.
* Drive capability and talent planning: hiring, onboarding, upskilling/certifications, and building communities of practice.
* Establish a clear operating rhythm across sites (delivery governance, staffing strategy, quality gates, and performance follow-up).
* Collaborate with Head of Integrations and APIs in strategy setting and aligning development of the subpractice.
* Take a leading role in transforming Capgemini’s Integration & API capability for the AI Agentic era, where integrations are no longer only system-to-system, but agent-to-agent, agent-to-system, and event-driven by AI.

**Ambassador & market maker for Integration and APIs**


* Act as a visible spokesperson for Integration & API: promote the offering internally (cross-business collaboration) and externally (events, client briefings, thought leadership content).
* Work closely with Capgemini’s partner representatives to develop partnerships and co-selling motions; strengthen ecosystem collaboration.

**Build pipeline through proactive presales & bids**


* Collaborate closely with the Integration CoE in creating pipeline via proactive client engagement and structured presales.
* Contribute with the integration perspective into bids: solution shaping, estimations, staffing approach, delivery model, risk management and value proposition.

**Client-facing advisory and delivery leadership**


* Engage confidently with clients and senior stakeholders, present integration strategies, architectures, and roadmaps with executive presence.
* Lead billable deliveries part-time, guide delivery teams to outcomes with strong governance and predictable execution.
* Shape client services in line with Head of Integration and APIs’ visions such as integration assessment & strategy, integration foundation/reference architecture, and delivery-at-scale.
  
***In this role you will play a key role in:***


* Lead and grow the Integration Subpractice in Sweden, in close collaboration with the Head of Integrations & APIs, ensuring strategic alignment and coordinated development of the subpractice.
* Acting as an ambassador and market maker for Integration and API driven solutions.
* Driving pipeline growth through proactive pre-sales activities and bid work.
* Providing client facing advisory support and leading delivery engagements.

**Your profile**


* Proven leadership experience as a people manager, ideally including manager-of-managers responsibility (leading team managers).
* Track record of building high-performing teams through coaching, development planning, recruitment support, and stakeholder management across sites.
* Experience or strong interest in applying *agentic AI concepts* within modern integration landscapes. Understanding how Agentic AI enhances integration platforms by enabling autonomous orchestration, intelligent observability, and proactive issue resolution—shaping more adaptive, scalable, and resilient integration landscapes.
* Extensive hands-on experience in integration architecture & solution design across hybrid environments (cloud + SaaS + on-prem).
* Strong experience with hyperscalers: Azure, AWS and/or GCP (integration services, patterns, security, governance) and SaaS/iPaaS integration platforms such as Boomi, MuleSoft, Workato. Certifications in any of these are beneficial.
* Experience with open-source integration variants is highly valued.
* IBM integration platform knowledge is a plus.
* Ability to align and collaborate across business lines and internal ecosystems, strong stakeholder management.
* Excellent communication in English; Swedish is highly valuable for many local client engagements.
* Relevant degree or equivalent experience.
**.**
-----


**What we believe you will enjoy about working here**


* You will get the opportunity to lead and shape our national integration subpractice.
* Work with leading clients and build a strong integration community with partners and hyperscalers.
* You will combine leadership with meaningful involvement in client engagements, ensuring you stay close to architecture, technology, and real-world value creation.
* Collaborate directly with leading hyperscalers and technology partners, co‑create solutions, and shape the next generation of integration capabilities.

**Application**


*We look forward to receiving your application and will review candidates on an ongoing basis. As part of our recruitment process, identity verification will be conducted through inspection of your ID card, and background checks may be carried out as required.*


**About Capgemini**  

Capgemini is an AI-powered global business and technology transformation partner, delivering tangible business value. We imagine the future of organizations and make it real with AI, technology and people. With our strong heritage of nearly 60 years, we are a responsible and diverse group of 420,000 team members in more than 50 countries. We deliver end-to-end services and solutions with our deep industry expertise and strong partner ecosystem, leveraging our capabilities across strategy, technology, design, engineering and business operations. The Group reported 2024 global revenues of €22.1 billion.


**Make it real |** **www.capgemini.com**
